title: First-Degree Burn (Superficial)
immediate_steps: Run cool (not cold) water over the burn for 10-15 minutes or until pain eases
Remove tight items from the burned area
Apply a soothing lotion (like aloe vera)
Cover loosely with a sterile bandage if needed
Take over-the-counter pain relievers if necessary
avoid: Do not apply ice
Do not apply butter or greasy ointments
Do not use adhesive bandages tightly

title: Second-Degree Burn (Partial Thickness)
symptoms: Red, dark red, or blotchy skin
immediate_steps: Cool the burn with cool running water for 15-20 minutes
Cover the burn loosely with a sterile, non-stick dressing
Elevate the burned area above heart level if possible
Seek medical attention if the burn is larger than 3 inches or on the face, hands, feet, groin, or over a major joint
avoid: Do not pop or break blisters
Do not peel off dead skin
Do not apply ice, creams, or home remedies like toothpaste
Do not use cotton balls which can stick to the wound

title: Third-Degree Burn (Full Thickness)
symptoms: Charred black, brown, or white skin
Numbness (no pain because nerve endings are destroyed)
immediate_steps: Call emergency services immediately
Ensure the person is no longer in contact with smoldering materials
Check for breathing and begin CPR if necessary
Cover the burn lightly with a sterile, non-stick bandage or clean cloth
Elevate the burned area
Treat for shock by laying the person flat and elevating feet
avoid: Do not remove clothing that is stuck to the burn
Do not submerge large severe burns in cold water (can cause hypothermia)
Do not apply any ointments or creams

title: Chemical Burn
symptoms: Redness, irritation, or burning at the site of contact
immediate_steps: Brush off any dry chemicals with a gloved hand or cloth
Flush the affected area with a steady stream of cool water for at least 20 minutes
Remove contaminated clothing and jewelry while flushing
Call emergency services or Poison Control
If in the eye, flush from the inner corner to the outer corner
avoid: Do not use water if the chemical reacts strongly with water (check material safety data sheet if possible)
Do not try to neutralize the chemical (e.g., using acid on an alkali)
Do not touch the chemical with bare hands

title: Burns - Types and Immediate Action
symptoms: White, charred, or leathery skin (3rd degree)
immediate_steps: Remove the person from the source of heat safely
Cool the burned area with running cool water for at least 10 to 20 minutes
Remove tight clothing or accessories if they are not stuck to the skin
avoid: Do not use ice directly on burns

title: Burns - Do's and Don'ts
immediate_steps: Do cool the burn immediately with clean running water for 10 to 20 minutes
Do cover the burn with a clean, non-stick cloth or sterile dressing
Do seek medical help for severe or large burns
avoid: Do not apply ice directly on the burn as it can worsen tissue damage
Do not apply toothpaste, oil, butter, or home remedies on burns
Do not break blisters as they protect against infection
Do not remove clothing stuck to the burned skin

title: Burns - Chemical and Electrical Burns
immediate_steps: For chemical burns: Immediately flush the affected area with large amounts of clean water for at least 15 to 20 minutes and remove contaminated clothing carefully
For electrical burns: Switch off the power supply first if possible
Electrical burns may cause internal injuries even if external damage appears minor, so immediate medical attention is necessary in all cases
avoid: Avoid rubbing the area of a chemical burn
Do not touch the victim if they are still in contact with the electrical source

title: Burns - Recovery and Infection Prevention
symptoms: Increased redness, swelling, pus, or fever
immediate_steps: Keep the burned area clean and covered with a sterile, non-stick dressing
Change dressings regularly as advised by medical professionals
Watch for signs of infection such as increased redness, swelling, pus, or fever
Severe burns require continuous medical supervision to prevent complications such as dehydration, shock, or scarring
avoid: Do not apply any unverified ointments or traditional remedies
